description: A water-repelling substance with the characteristic flow and slippery feel of oil is 'loesi'. Some oils thicken or set in cool conditions; their source may be plant or animal, mineral or synthetic. 'larewu' (liquid) is the wider phase word. Only the particular oil tells whether it belongs near food or machinery, a lamp or bodily care.
articulatory_notes: Lateral 'l' releases into rounded 'o'. The voice crosses a clear hiatus into stressed front 'e'; a narrow tongue groove then directs unvoiced 's' into final high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: Source and intended use can come before 'loesi'. A product label supplies formulation or safety information when the broad noun cannot.

semantic_domains.creation: a material used in food, lamps, and lubrication
semantic_domains.physical: a water-repelling substance with characteristic flow and texture
